lines: Settle into your position for sleep. [long pause] Take a moment to adjust anything that needs adjusting. [long pause] Your pillow — higher, lower, flatter, whatever feels right. [long pause] Your blanket — pulled up or pushed down. [long pause] The way your body lies against the mattress. [long pause] Make yourself comfortable. [long pause] Make yourself at home in this bed.

Notice the points where your body makes contact with the surface beneath you. [long pause] The back of your head, cradled by the pillow. [long pause] Your shoulder blades pressing gently into the mattress. [long pause] Your lower back. [long pause] The backs of your arms. [long pause] Your calves. [long pause] Your heels. [long pause] Wherever you feel contact, let your weight sink a little more into that place. [long pause] Let the mattress receive you. [long pause] It's been waiting all day for this.

Let your eyes close. [long pause] Let your jaw soften — let it hang open just slightly, so your teeth aren't touching. [long pause] Let your tongue drop from the roof of your mouth. [long pause] Let your hands fall open at your sides. [long pause] Let your fingers uncurl.

Tonight, you don't need to do anything. [long pause] You don't need to solve anything. [long pause] You don't need to plan or review or decide. [long pause] You just need to breathe. [long pause] And even that, your body will do for you.

Take one deep, unstructured breath in through your nose... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] fill your whole chest and belly... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and release it slowly through your mouth with a long sigh.

Again. [long pause] In through the nose, deeply... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and out through the mouth with a sigh. [long pause] Let the exhale carry some of today's weight away from you.
One more. [long pause] Deep breath in... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and let everything ride out on the exhale. [long pause] Good.

Tonight we breathe with the Psalms. [long pause] The Psalms are the oldest prayers in the faith — they've been on human lips for three thousand years. [long pause] Whispered in temples, chanted in cathedrals, cried out in desert nights, murmured in hospital rooms. [long pause] They are honest prayers. [long pause] They don't pretend everything is fine. [long pause] They rage and weep and doubt and praise and beg and surrender. [long pause] And tonight, three of them will become the rhythm of your breath.

We'll use a practice called coherent breathing — a slow, even rhythm that research has shown calms the autonomic nervous system and brings the heart, lungs, and brain into sync. [long pause] About five and a half seconds in, five and a half seconds out. [long pause] You don't need to count precisely. [long pause] I'll pace you with the words, and your body will find the rhythm.
Our first psalm phrase comes from Psalm sixty-two. [long pause] David, running from enemies, hunted and afraid, writes this: "Truly my soul finds rest in God alone."
Not in safety. [long pause] Not in certainty. [long pause] Not in having everything figured out. [long pause] In God alone.

We'll split the verse across the breath. [long pause] The first half rides the inhale. [long pause] The second half rides the exhale. [long pause] Let the words become the breath. [long pause] Let the breath become the prayer.
Breathe in slowly with me now: "My soul finds rest... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] "
And breathe out slowly: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] in God alone."
Breathe in: "My soul finds rest... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] "
And out: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] in God alone."
In: "My soul finds rest... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] "
Out: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] in God alone."
In: "My soul finds rest... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] "
Out: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] in God alone."
In: "My soul finds rest... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] "
Out: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] in God alone."
In: "My soul finds rest... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] "
Out: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] in God alone."
Now continue that rhythm on your own. [long pause] The same words. [long pause] The same gentle pace. [long pause] I'll be quiet, and you just keep breathing the prayer.
My soul finds rest... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] in God alone.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

Good. [long pause] Let those words settle into your body. [long pause] Feel how the rhythm has already begun to change things. [long pause] Your heart rate is slowing, synchronizing with your breath. [long pause] Your nervous system is receiving the message, over and over: you are safe. [long pause] You can rest. [long pause] You are not alone.
Let that phrase dissolve now. [long pause] Like mist burning off in morning light. [long pause] The words go, but the peace they carried stays.

Our next phrase comes from Psalm one hundred and thirty-one. [long pause] It's one of the shortest psalms — only three verses. [long pause] And one of the most tender. [long pause] The psalmist writes: "I have calmed and quieted my soul; like a weaned child with its mother, like a weaned child is my soul within me."

A weaned child. [long pause] Think about what that means. [long pause] Not a hungry infant, crying for milk, needing something urgently. [long pause] A child who has been fed. [long pause] Who has no craving left. [long pause] Who simply rests against its mother's chest because it wants to be near her. [long pause] Content. [long pause] Still. [long pause] At home. [long pause] Wanting nothing except what it already has.
Breathe in: "I have calmed... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] "
Breathe out: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and quieted my soul."
In: "I have calmed... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] "
Out: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and quieted my soul."
In: "I have calmed... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] "
Out: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and quieted my soul."
In: "I have calmed... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] "
Out: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and quieted my soul."
In: "I have calmed... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] "
Out: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and quieted my soul."
In: "I have calmed... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] "
Out: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and quieted my soul."
Now on your own. [long pause] Keep the same pace. [long pause] Let the words move with your breath like a boat rocking gently on still water.
I have calmed... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and quieted my soul.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

You are that child. [long pause] Not reaching for anything. [long pause] Not grasping for the next thing. [long pause] Not scrolling, not planning, not rehearsing tomorrow. [long pause] Just resting. [long pause] Content. [long pause] Held against the chest of someone who has already given you everything you need for tonight.

Let that phrase dissolve. [long pause] A new one arrives now. [long pause] From Psalm forty-six, verse ten. [long pause] Perhaps the most direct invitation to stillness in all of Scripture: "Be still, and know that I am God."

Be still. [long pause] Not "be productive." Not "be ready." Not "figure it out." Be still. [long pause] The Hebrew word is raphah — it means to let go, to release your grip, to stop striving. [long pause] It's a command to do... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] nothing. [long pause] The only command in the Bible that asks less of you, not more.
Breathe in: "Be still... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] "
Breathe out: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and know."
Out: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and know."
Out: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and know."
Out: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and know."
Out: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and know."
Out: "... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and know."
Continue on your own. [long pause] Be still... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and know.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

Now let the words begin to dissolve. [long pause] All three phrases — rest, quiet, still — they've done their work. [long pause] They've seeped into your breath, your blood, your bones. [long pause] You don't need them anymore. [long pause] Just keep the rhythm. [long pause] Five seconds in. [long pause] Five seconds out. [long pause] Slow, even, steady. [long pause] No words. [long pause] Just breath. [long pause] The words live in the rhythm now.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

Your awareness begins to soften and spread through your body. [long pause] Don't follow it with effort — don't try to direct it. [long pause] Just let it move on its own, like water flowing downhill, finding its own path through the landscape of your body.

Awareness in your right hand. [long pause] Feel it from the inside. [long pause] The thumb — its weight, its warmth. [long pause] The index finger. [long pause] The middle finger. [long pause] The ring finger. [long pause] The little finger. [long pause] The palm of your right hand — the lines, the warmth, the pulse of blood beneath the skin. [long pause] Your whole right hand, heavy and warm and alive.

Awareness in your left hand. [long pause] The thumb. [long pause] The index finger. [long pause] The middle finger. [long pause] The ring finger. [long pause] The little finger. [long pause] The palm of your left hand. [long pause] Warm and heavy. [long pause] Both hands now, resting open, holding nothing. [long pause] Twin weights of stillness.

Awareness in your right foot. [long pause] The big toe. [long pause] The second toe. [long pause] The third. [long pause] The fourth. [long pause] The little toe. [long pause] The ball of the foot. [long pause] The arch. [long pause] The heel. [long pause] The sole of your right foot — the surface that meets the world, step after step. [long pause] Now at rest. [long pause] Warm and still.

Awareness in your left foot. [long pause] The big toe. [long pause] The second toe. [long pause] The third. [long pause] The fourth. [long pause] The little toe. [long pause] The ball of the foot. [long pause] The arch. [long pause] The heel. [long pause] The sole of your left foot. [long pause] Warm and still. [long pause] Both feet now. [long pause] Grounded, even in bed. [long pause] Connected to the earth beneath you through layers of mattress and floor and foundation and soil.

Now the whole right side of your body. [long pause] From the right side of your head, the right ear, your right shoulder, your right arm, your right hand, the right side of your ribcage, your right hip, your right leg, your right foot. [long pause] One warm, heavy, continuous line from top to bottom.

The whole left side of your body. [long pause] Left ear, left shoulder, left arm, left hand, left ribs, left hip, left leg, left foot. [long pause] One warm, heavy line. [long pause] Mirror image. [long pause] Balanced. [long pause] At rest.

The back of your body — everything that presses into the bed. [long pause] The back of your head. [long pause] Your shoulder blades. [long pause] Your spine, that long faithful column. [long pause] The backs of your arms. [long pause] Your lower back. [long pause] The backs of your thighs. [long pause] Your calves. [long pause] Your heels. [long pause] One long surface of contact with the bed. [long pause] Supported. [long pause] Held up.

The front of your body. [long pause] Your forehead. [long pause] Your face. [long pause] Your throat. [long pause] Your chest, rising and falling. [long pause] Your belly, soft and round. [long pause] The fronts of your thighs. [long pause] Your shins. [long pause] The tops of your feet. [long pause] Open and soft. [long pause] Unguarded. [long pause] Vulnerable in the best way — the way a sleeping person is vulnerable. [long pause] Trusting.

The whole body now. [long pause] One body. [long pause] One breath. [long pause] One rhythm. [long pause] No division between left and right, front and back, head and feet. [long pause] Just wholeness. [long pause] Just the entirety of you, breathing. [long pause] Here. [long pause] Alive. [long pause] At rest.

God, these ancient words have carried people to sleep for thousands of years. [long pause] Kings and shepherds. [long pause] Prisoners and pilgrims. [long pause] The afraid and the grateful. [long pause] Tonight they carried me. [long pause] My soul finds rest in you alone. [long pause] I have calmed and quieted my soul. [long pause] I will be still and know that you are God. [long pause] That is enough for tonight. [long pause] That is everything tonight. [long pause] Amen.

The breath continues on its own now. [long pause] You don't need to pace it. [long pause] You don't need to guide it. [long pause] It knows the rhythm. [long pause] It will carry it through the night.
My soul finds rest... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] in God alone.
I have calmed... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and quieted my soul.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]
